lines.gurmukhi: ਦਯਿ ਵਿਗੋਏ ਫਿਰਹਿ ਵਿਗੁਤੇ ਫਿਟਾ ਵਤੈ ਗਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: day vigoe fireh vigute fittaa vatai galaa |
lines.english: Ruined by the Merciful Lord, they wander around in disgrace, and their entire troop is contaminated.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਆ ਮਾਰਿ ਜੀਵਾਲੇ ਸੋਈ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਕੋਈ ਰਖੈ ॥
lines.translit: jeea maar jeevaale soee avar na koee rakhai |
lines.english: The Lord alone kills and restores to life; no one else can protect anyone from Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਣੀ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਰਤਨ ਉਪੰਨੇ ਮੇਰੁ ਕੀਆ ਮਾਧਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: paanee vichahu ratan upane mer keea maadhaanee |
lines.english: The jewel emerged from the water, when the mountain of gold was used to churn it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਾਨਹੁ ਤੈ ਇਸਨਾਨਹੁ ਵੰਜੇ ਭਸੁ ਪਈ ਸਿਰਿ ਖੁਥੈ ॥
lines.translit: daanahu tai isanaanahu vanje bhas pee sir khuthai |
lines.english: They go without giving alms or any cleansing baths; their shaven heads become covered with dust.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਠਸਠਿ ਤੀਰਥ ਦੇਵੀ ਥਾਪੇ ਪੁਰਬੀ ਲਗੈ ਬਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: atthasatth teerath devee thaape purabee lagai baanee |
lines.english: The gods established the sixty-eight sacred shrines of pilgrimage, where the festivals are celebrated and hymns are chanted.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਇ ਨਿਵਾਜਾ ਨਾਤੈ ਪੂਜਾ ਨਾਵਨਿ ਸਦਾ ਸੁਜਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: naae nivaajaa naatai poojaa naavan sadaa sujaanee |
lines.english: After bathing, the Muslims recite their prayers, and after bathing, the Hindus perform their worship services. The wise always take cleansing baths.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਿਰਖੁਥੇ ਸੈਤਾਨੀ ਏਨਾ ਗਲ ਨ ਭਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: naanak sirakhuthe saitaanee enaa gal na bhaanee |
lines.english: O Nanak, the shaven-headed ones are devils. They are not pleased to hear these words.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁਇਆ ਜੀਵਦਿਆ ਗਤਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਜਾਂ ਸਿਰਿ ਪਾਈਐ ਪਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: mueaa jeevadiaa gat hovai jaan sir paaeeai paanee |
lines.english: At the time of death, and at the time of birth, they are purified, when water is poured on their heads.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵੁਠੈ ਘਾਹੁ ਚਰਹਿ ਨਿਤਿ ਸੁਰਹੀ ਸਾ ਧਨ ਦਹੀ ਵਿਲੋਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: vutthai ghaahu chareh nit surahee saa dhan dahee vilovai |
lines.english: When it rains, the cows always have grass to graze upon, and housewives can churn the milk into butter.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵੁਠੈ ਹੋਇਐ ਹੋਇ ਬਿਲਾਵਲੁ ਜੀਆ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਸਮਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: vutthai hoeiai hoe bilaaval jeea jugat samaanee |
lines.english: When it rains, there is happiness. Water is the key to all life.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵੁਠੈ ਅੰਨੁ ਕਮਾਦੁ ਕਪਾਹਾ ਸਭਸੈ ਪੜਦਾ ਹੋਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: vutthai an kamaad kapaahaa sabhasai parradaa hovai |
lines.english: When it rains, the corn grows, and the sugar cane, and the cotton, which provides clothing for all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰੂ ਸਮੁੰਦੁ ਨਦੀ ਸਭਿ ਸਿਖੀ ਨਾਤੈ ਜਿਤੁ ਵਡਿਆਈ ॥
lines.translit: guroo samund nadee sabh sikhee naatai jit vaddiaaee |
lines.english: The Guru is the ocean, and all His Teachings are the river. Bathing within it, glorious greatness is obtained.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਤੁ ਘਿਇ ਹੋਮ ਜਗ ਸਦ ਪੂਜਾ ਪਇਐ ਕਾਰਜੁ ਸੋਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: tit ghie hom jag sad poojaa peiai kaaraj sohai |
lines.english: With that ghee, sacred feasts and worship services are performed; all these efforts are blessed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜੇ ਸਿਰਖੁਥੇ ਨਾਵਨਿ ਨਾਹੀ ਤਾ ਸਤ ਚਟੇ ਸਿਰਿ ਛਾਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak je sirakhuthe naavan naahee taa sat chatte sir chhaaee |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, if the shaven-headed ones do not bathe, then seven handfuls of ashes are upon their heads. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗੀ ਪਾਲਾ ਕਿ ਕਰੇ ਸੂਰਜ ਕੇਹੀ ਰਾਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: agee paalaa ki kare sooraj kehee raat |
lines.english: What can the cold do to the fire? How can the night affect the sun?

lines.gurmukhi: ਚੰਦ ਅਨੇਰਾ ਕਿ ਕਰੇ ਪਉਣ ਪਾਣੀ ਕਿਆ ਜਾਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: chand aneraa ki kare paun paanee kiaa jaat |
lines.english: What can the darkness do to the moon? What can social status do to air and water?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਤਾ ਪਤਿ ਜਾਣੀਐ ਜਾ ਪਤਿ ਰਖੈ ਸੋਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak taa pat jaaneeai jaa pat rakhai soe |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, he alone is known as honorable, whose honor the Lord preserves. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਰਤੀ ਚੀਜੀ ਕਿ ਕਰੇ ਜਿਸੁ ਵਿਚਿ ਸਭੁ ਕਿਛੁ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: dharatee cheejee ki kare jis vich sabh kichh hoe |
lines.english: What are personal possessions to the earth, from which all things are produced?

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਜਿ ਮੰਗਹਿ ਦਾਨੁ ਸਿ ਤੁਧੈ ਜੇਹਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: sach ji mangeh daan si tudhai jehiaa |
lines.english: Those who ask for the gift of the True Name are like You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂੰ ਸਚਾ ਦੀਬਾਣੁ ਹੋਰਿ ਆਵਣ ਜਾਣਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: toon sachaa deebaan hor aavan jaaniaa |
lines.english: Yours is the True Court. All others are subject to coming and going.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਧੁ ਸਚੇ ਸੁਬਹਾਨੁ ਸਦਾ ਕਲਾਣਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: tudh sache subahaan sadaa kalaaniaa |
lines.english: It is of You, O my True and Wondrous Lord, that I sing forever.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੰਨਿਐ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਤੁਧੈ ਤੇ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: maniai giaan dhiaan tudhai te paaeaa |
lines.english: Through faith and trust, we receive spiritual wisdom and meditation from You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਫੁਰਮਾਨੁ ਸਬਦੇ ਸੋਹਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: sach teraa furamaan sabade sohiaa |
lines.english: Your Command is True; we are adorned with the Word of Your Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਮਿ ਪਵੈ ਨੀਸਾਨੁ ਨ ਚਲੈ ਚਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: karam pavai neesaan na chalai chalaaeaa |
lines.english: By Your Grace, the banner of honor is obtained. It cannot be taken away or lost.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂੰ ਸਚਾ ਦਾਤਾਰੁ ਨਿਤ ਦੇਵਹਿ ਚੜਹਿ ਸਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: toon sachaa daataar nit deveh charreh savaaeaa |
lines.english: You are the True Giver; You give continually. Your Gifts continue to increase.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕੁ ਮੰਗੈ ਦਾਨੁ ਜੋ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਇਆ ॥੨੬॥
lines.translit: naanak mangai daan jo tudh bhaaeaa |26|
lines.english: Nanak begs for that gift which is pleasing to You. ||26||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੀਖਿਆ ਆਖਿ ਬੁਝਾਇਆ ਸਿਫਤੀ ਸਚਿ ਸਮੇਉ ॥
lines.translit: deekhiaa aakh bujhaaeaa sifatee sach sameo |
lines.english: Those who have accepted the Guru's Teachings, and who have found the path, remain absorbed in the Praises of the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਕਉ ਕਿਆ ਉਪਦੇਸੀਐ ਜਿਨ ਗੁਰੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਦੇਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: tin kau kiaa upadeseeai jin gur naanak deo |1|
lines.english: What teachings can be imparted to those who have the Divine Guru Nanak as their Guru? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Salok, Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਬੁਝਾਏ ਸੋਈ ਬੂਝੈ ॥
lines.translit: aap bujhaae soee boojhai |
lines.english: We understand the Lord only when He Himself inspires us to understand Him.

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਆਪਿ ਸੁਝਾਏ ਤਿਸੁ ਸਭੁ ਕਿਛੁ ਸੂਝੈ ॥
lines.translit: jis aap sujhaae tis sabh kichh soojhai |
lines.english: He alone knows everything, unto whom the Lord Himself gives knowledge.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮੀ ਸਗਲ ਕਰੇ ਆਕਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: hukamee sagal kare aakaar |
lines.english: The Lord, by the Hukam of His Command, has created the entire creation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਕਹਿ ਕਥਨਾ ਮਾਇਆ ਲੂਝੈ ॥
lines.translit: keh keh kathanaa maaeaa loojhai |
lines.english: One may talk and preach and give sermons but still yearn after Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਜਾਣੈ ਸਰਬ ਵੀਚਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: aape jaanai sarab veechaar |
lines.english: He Himself knows the inner nature of all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਖਰ ਨਾਨਕ ਅਖਿਓ ਆਪਿ ॥
lines.translit: akhar naanak akhio aap |
lines.english: O Nanak, He Himself uttered the Word.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਢਾਢੀ ਵੇਕਾਰੁ ਕਾਰੈ ਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: hau dtaadtee vekaar kaarai laaeaa |
lines.english: I was a minstrel, out of work, when the Lord took me into His service.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਹੈ ਭਰਾਤਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਜਿਸੁ ਦਾਤਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: lahai bharaat hovai jis daat |2|
lines.english: Doubt departs from one who receives this gift. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੀ ਸਿਫਤਿ ਸਾਲਾਹ ਕਪੜਾ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: sachee sifat saalaah kaparraa paaeaa |
lines.english: He has dressed me in the robes of His True Praise and Glory.

lines.gurmukhi: ਢਾਢੀ ਸਚੈ ਮਹਲਿ ਖਸਮਿ ਬੁਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: dtaadtee sachai mehal khasam bulaaeaa |
lines.english: My Lord and Master has summoned me, His minstrel, to the True Mansion of His Presence.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਤਿ ਦਿਹੈ ਕੈ ਵਾਰ ਧੁਰਹੁ ਫੁਰਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: raat dihai kai vaar dhurahu furamaaeaa |
lines.english: To sing His Praises day and night, He gave me His Order, right from the start.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚਾ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਾਮੁ ਭੋਜਨੁ ਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: sachaa amrit naam bhojan aaeaa |
lines.english: The Ambrosial Nectar of the True Name has become my food.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮਤੀ ਖਾਧਾ ਰਜਿ ਤਿਨਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: guramatee khaadhaa raj tin sukh paaeaa |
lines.english: Those who follow the Guru's Teachings, who eat this food and are satisfied, find peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਚੁ ਸਾਲਾਹਿ ਪੂਰਾ ਪਾਇਆ ॥੨੭॥ ਸੁਧੁ
lines.translit: naanak sach saalaeh pooraa paaeaa |27| sudh
lines.english: O Nanak, praising the True Lord, I have obtained His Perfection. ||27||Sudh||

lines.gurmukhi: ਢਾਢੀ ਕਰੇ ਪਸਾਉ ਸਬਦੁ ਵਜਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: dtaadtee kare pasaau sabad vajaaeaa |
lines.english: His minstrel spreads His Glory, singing and vibrating the Word of His Shabad.
